title: Dim an LED with a Potentiometer
description: Adjust LED brightness using a potentiometer and an Arduino.

dialogue.text: Orion here again! Ready to add some interactivity? We'll use a potentiometer as a dimmer for your LED. Work on a non-conductive surface, touch grounded metal to discharge static, keep your hands dry, and unplug the Arduino while wiring to avoid shorts.

options.text: Sounds good! What do I need?

dialogue.text: Gather one Arduino Uno, a breadboard, three jumper wires, one 5 mm LED, one 220 Ω resistor, one potentiometer, one USB cable, and a laptop with the Arduino IDE installed. Keep parts organized so leads don't touch.

Disconnect the USB cable. Place the potentiometer on the breadboard. Wire its side pins to 5 V and GND and the middle wiper to A0. Keep the LED on pin 9 through a 220 Ω resistor to limit current. Double-check that no bare wires touch before reconnecting power.

Reconnect the USB cable, open the Arduino IDE, and load a sketch that reads A0 and maps the value to analogWrite on pin 9 to dim the LED. Avoid staring into the LED at full brightness and keep liquids away from the board while it's powered.

options.text: LED fades as I turn the knob.

dialogue.text: Great job! Disconnect power before changing the circuit and store the parts for future projects.
